sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 860400, "Logan House Creek", Stream, NV, Douglas Co.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":860400,"gaz_name":"Logan House Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"NV","county_name":"Douglas"} — geometry multipoint; mouth point (Web Mercator -13352052.5929, 4731372.3910) converts to lon -119.94353, lat 39.06769, about 211 m from the file's recorded coordinate (-119.94144, 39.06671) and closely matching the USGS streamgage 10336745 location (lat 39.06768587, lon -119.942405, dec_coord_datum_cd NAD83) at the creek's mouth on Lake Tahoe near Glenbrook — confirms the file's coordinate is accurate within tolerance.

sources.title: Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners, CR 25-16, Fishing Seasons and Regulations (Jan. 1, 2026 – Dec. 31, 2027) — Douglas County, "Lake Tahoe and tributaries" listing
sources.quotedText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around. Daily: 5 game fish. Possession: 10 game fish. Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

sources.title: CR 25-16 — Douglas County CLOSED WATERS (Logan House Creek not listed as closed; only Glenbrook Creek is)
sources.quotedText: Topaz Lake within the jetties of Topaz Marina. Lake Tahoe within the boat launch area, inside the jetty at the Cave Rock boat ramp, north end. Glenbrook Creek from Lake Tahoe to Highway 50.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

sources.title: USGS 10336745 — LOGAN HOUSE CK AT LAKE TAHOE NR GLENBROOK, NV (streamgage at the creek's mouth on Lake Tahoe, Douglas Co., NV)
sources.quotedText: LOGAN HOUSE CK AT LAKE TAHOE NR GLENBROOK, NV

sources.title: Lake Tahoe Watershed Assessment, Volume I (USDA Forest Service Gen. Tech. Rep. PSW-GTR-175) — east shore tributaries (Glenbrook and Logan House)
sources.quotedText: The east shore tributaries (Glenbrook and Logan House) had an order of magnitude lower sediment loading than the west shore tributaries (Ward, Blackwood, and General), the northeast shore tributaries (Third and Incline), or the Upper Truckee River.

parameters.note: Logan House Creek is an east-shore Lake Tahoe tributary lying entirely within Douglas County, Nevada; it flows west into Lake Tahoe near Glenbrook (USGS gage 10336745 is named 'Logan House Ck at Lake Tahoe nr Glenbrook, NV'; the USFS Lake Tahoe Watershed Assessment lists 'the east shore tributaries (Glenbrook and Logan House)' among the Lake Tahoe Interagency Monitoring Program watersheds). It is not named individually in CR 25-16, so the applicable Douglas County listing is 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' — open year around. Logan House Creek is NOT in Douglas County's closed-waters list (only Glenbrook Creek is), so it is open all year. The California Lake Tahoe tributary catch-and-release closure (CCR T14 §7.50(b)(152)) does not reach this creek: that special regulation applies only to Tahoe tributaries in Placer and El Dorado counties, California — not to Nevada-side tributaries, which NDOW governs. (A separate mapped tributary, North Logan House Creek, joins near the mouth; both lie in Douglas County and share the same 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' treatment.)

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Douglas County — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Open year-round (Nevada / Douglas County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing; Logan House Creek is a Lake Tahoe tributary and is not in the county's closed-waters list).
regulations.verbatimText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around.

parameters.counting_rule: Douglas County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' limit: 5 game fish daily, 10 game fish in possession. 'Game fish' is the combined coldwater/warmwater game-fish count; no species sub-limit is listed for this row in CR 25-16.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Douglas County — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: 5 game fish per day, 10 in possession (Nevada Douglas County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' limit).
regulations.verbatimText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Daily: 5 game fish. Possession: 10 game fish.

parameters.note: The 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' row carries restricted hours (1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset) — narrower than Douglas County's general 'All waters' rule of any hour of the day or night.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Douglas County — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing permitted from 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

parameters.note: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers 12 and older. Logan House Creek is a Nevada tributary (Douglas Co.), not the open water of Lake Tahoe; the Lake Tahoe CA/NV reciprocal-license arrangement is written for fishing anywhere ON the lake, so a Nevada license (not a California license alone) should be carried on the creek.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; none required under 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
